ddls:
CREATE TABLE "literary_works" (
  "work_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each literary work */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the literary work */,
  "body" TEXT /* Full text of the literary work */,
  "pub_date" TEXT /* Publication date of the work */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the work */,
  "sub_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sub-category of the work */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who submitted the work */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the work (e.g., submitted, published, in review) */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the work */,
  "word_count" INTEGER /* Word count of the work */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the work */,
  "isbn" TEXT /* ISBN of the published work */,
  "sales_data" TEXT /* Sales data for the published work */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("work_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_literary_works_sub_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("sub_category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_literary_works_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_literary_works_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES authors ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each author */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biography of the author */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the author */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the author */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the author */,
  "date_of_death" TEXT /* Date of death of the author (if applicable) */,
  "website" TEXT /* Author's personal website or social media */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Fiction, Non-Fiction) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent category */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the category is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "editors" (
  "editor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each editor */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the editor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the editor */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the editor (e.g., senior editor, junior editor) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the editor */,
  "date_hired" TEXT /* Date the editor was hired */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the editor's manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("editor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_editors_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES editors ("editor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the literary work being reviewed */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reviewer */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the reviewer (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment or feedback provided by the reviewer */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the review was submitted */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the review (e.g., professional critic, reader) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL to the review if published elsewhere */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES readers ("reader_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_work_id FOREIGN KEY ("work_id") REFERENCES literary_works ("work_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readers" (
  "reader_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reader */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the reader */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the reader */,
  "preferences" TEXT /* Preferences or interests of the reader */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date the reader joined the platform */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Date of the reader's last activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reader_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "publication_histories" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each publication history record */,
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the literary work */,
  "pub_date" TEXT /* Date of publication */,
  "editor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the editor responsible for publication */,
  "status" TEXT /* Publication status (e.g., published, pending) */,
  "isbn" TEXT /* ISBN of the published work */,
  "edition" TEXT /* Edition of the published work */,
  "publisher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the publisher responsible for the publication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_publication_histories_work_id FOREIGN KEY ("work_id") REFERENCES literary_works ("work_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_publication_histories_editor_id FOREIGN KEY ("editor_id") REFERENCES editors ("editor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_materials" (
  "material_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing material */,
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated literary work */,
  "material_type" TEXT /* Type of marketing material (e.g., cover image, brochure) */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the marketing material */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the material was uploaded */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the marketing material */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the marketing material (e.g., active, archived) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("material_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_marketing_materials_work_id FOREIGN KEY ("work_id") REFERENCES literary_works ("work_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the literary work being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the work */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the work was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the work */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_work_id FOREIGN KEY ("work_id") REFERENCES literary_works ("work_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., author, editor, reviewer) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_accounts" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role assigned to the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of last login */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_accounts_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the notification was created */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the notification has been read */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., submission, review, publication) */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_accounts ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the literary work being commented on */,
  "reader_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reader making the comment */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was made */,
  "reply_to_comment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the comment this comment is a reply to */,
  "is_approved" INTEGER /* Indicates if the comment is approved for public display */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_reply_to_comment_id FOREIGN KEY ("reply_to_comment_id") REFERENCES comments ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_reader_id FOREIGN KEY ("reader_id") REFERENCES readers ("reader_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_work_id FOREIGN KEY ("work_id") REFERENCES literary_works ("work_id")
)
